steps.title: Identify Text 1's core assessment
steps.content: Text 1 characterizes Orlando as a minor work fundamentally different from Woolf's other major novels—fantastical rather than everyday, psychologically superficial rather than deeply interior, and dismissed by Woolf herself as “a joke.”

steps.title: Match Text 2's response
steps.content:
Orlando is central to Woolf's body of work because it engages seriously with the same literary themes as her classics, despite being lighter in tone.

The other responses misread Text 2 by either overstating the concession about Orlando's impact (A and B) or focusing on overlooking instead of thematic substance (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
